content: great until it stops working. every once in awhile i am "logged in" but i have to relog in. then i cannot due to a server issue. the website doesn't have this issue, but it does automatically redirect to the broken application when i select something. steam guard only compounds this problem. the desktop application and the website seem to be much better platforms.

in the old app, you could see friends online, and message them. now i need to use the inferior steam chat, a separate app? that's annoying. sure, the new ui is pretty, bit we haven't gained any functionality, we lost some. edit: the friends list is just a little buried. if like for it to be available as a tab at the bottom, as checking who is online is my most commonly used function when using this app.

app is nice to check out games when not at my desk, but i always have issues, it seems. app taking forever load even with good connection, half the screen being blocked by a grey bar, tapping on something multiple times before it opens, and a few other issues. it's been this way for at least a year, so i have no idea of or when these issues will be resolved. my internet speed is fine to, so i can only assume it's the app.

just doesn't work sometimes. presently can't set it as my 2 factor, it (from the app) tells me i need to install the app, and sends me to the play store ostensibly to install... itself, which of course isn't an option from the play store. if i try to click done, it just silently fails out. i eventually figured out that there is a second place i had to go to set up the 2 factor, which worked, but the ux was awful. presumably this is because most of the app is just a web browser.

it does what it's supposed to do. you can buy games, chat with friends and see your library. the only thing that is severely lacking on the mobile app version (at least on a samsung note 9) is the forums section. it is atrocious and unusable. all the words are overlapped; you can't see the topics. i hope this feature gets fixed becuase i use it a lot.

its okay but omg way way way too much going on everywhere. and the fact that there are 1000 options on individual friend profiles and none of them are to view their wishlist is weird to me. in general there is a lot going on without there being much method to it making it difficult to navigate. otherwise, it runs decent for me, there arent really any major issues that ive run into so far.

fix the android steam app. most of the games in the library doesn't have a cover page and no title is written under it making it difficult. always have to open the gray box to see the game name in your library. the app is very slow even with high speed internet connection let it be searching for games, loading the detail of the game from the search, image loading etc. everything is damn slow.

it's a good app for what it is and convenient to use. well, except for one small problem. paypal hasn't worked for who knows how long and i'm unable to purchase with it, forcing me to use steam in my browser instead, which is very annoying.

it's great to have installed for the sake of keeping your account secure and purchasing content when you're away from the pc. i do wish the application could be overhauled into something much better (more efficient and less of a clunky mess) in the future.

the app is great, but i dont know if its just my steam app, as i keep needing to sign in even though i did 50 times each time i leave the app, and then i cant add the steam guard thing as when i press the retry button, it just send me back to the steam guard and it keeps repeating. im just trying to recover my account for my pc since i dont wanna spend 21 pounds again just for a game!

its been logging me out constantly, can't connect to library, and says error occurred when adding this account to steam guard... which i've already setup in the past. basically acts like im not signed despite having just signed in.

steam as a platform is great, but the mobile app is very inconsistent. the library often fails to load with an ‘oops, something went wrong’ error. when it works, it’s useful. when it doesn’t, it’s frustrating. stability seriously needs improvement.

please improve the navigation so that the back button works correctly and consistently. please start opening game pages in new tabs or windows so that we can check out a title, and then return to the same place in a list. it's very clunky and inconsistent. you have almost infinite financial resources. do better.

the app itself is fine, clear menus and navigation. the issue lies with the occassional disconnects, which can be particularly annoying regarding the steamguard 2fa. (despite adjusting the app regarding the auto manage permission removal/deep sleep) the experience varies from no issues to being presented as logged off. logging in either solves the issue or validates the current session only (with occassional desyncs in the games & friends showing 0 or wrong numbers, fresh install required)
the changes to the ui and overall design is worse than it was a few years ago but it is what it is. the app runs well and does its job to protect trades and your account.

i'd give it 2½ stars if i could. i love stream, but recent update logged me out, which is fine, i logged back in easy enough, but whenever i open the app, the store doesn't load unless i hit refresh, even after closing the app completely.

generally works for shopping games, viewing your library, and trading market content. its "community" feature is full of pornographic content and there's basically no way to save yourself from it without blocking your ability to use the platform entirely.

love the game but customer service need serious help i bought a game they charged me but it said my card declined how is that possible and then they sent a email telling me i could purchase again with the price excluded but they charge me again and now i just lost 21.94 on helldiver's help me please!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

nice when it 'works' (signing in, etc) but for the longest time, in app using paypal, if it goes to"enter email etc" it just loops; i have to log in on chrome to make purchase. the app works sometimes.

if you want to check a particular item details on the steam community market by pressing on a particular item, it wont expand the item to show details of it anymore! i dont know is it just me or what, same on the browser in the steamcommunity page. :(

a much needed update finally happened. valve ditched html5 wrapper (mostly) and now it feels like a native app - faster and smoother. there are still some questionable design elements which clearly part of its history. hope they continue to regularly update this app and bring it closer to the functionally of the desktop version as much as possible. still feels pretty bad. a far cry from the actual steam app on windows.

there is one issue with the app. whenever i am inside a game page and let's say i want to go back, when i click the back button, the app closes. ideally it should go back to the home page from where i clicked on the game. fix this issue.

i can check the game price and discount when have an event.this app brings me a lot easier without using a laptop.but recently,the app loading menu is stuck and was like 1 weeks.please value,fix this problem.without this app,make me waste more time on laptop.
the steam support is responsive and quick! and i can download pc games on my desktop and its not running so well. so i'd rather rate this a 3 stars for the best!
interface is a bit hard to navigate sometimes. the video player/slider is broken in game details, it goes to the next item while playing a video, videos are laggy and stutter.
can't go back to home screen after clicking on one of the sales. android back button closes the app. restarting the app fixed but app seems to still be buggy.

i rlly don't get why games are available on ios but not on android. like what even is the reason behind this? i love steam, the pricing here is regional. but i can't purchase the games 'cuz i can't play em on mobile and it's annoying. pleasee make the games available on android!!

its ok, however when i was trying to look through a friend's library and i know it wasn't private, when i tapped the "filter to games we both own" button, it did literally nothing, i know it works on desktop but unlike 90% of steam users, im not at my desktop 24/7/365 rotting in a chair.

decent app, but please stop auto scrolling through game screenshots! i'm trying to admire the game developer's work and it's hard to do that when it auto scrolls every fews seconds. please, at least add a toggle in settings so i can turn the auto scrolling off.

don't personally understand why i need to link a phone number to be able to sign in with a qr code. i don't want a phone number on my account which i guess is a me problem, but it wasted a bit of my time.
good for adding games to your wishlist. doesn't appear to show adult content in recent events & announcements. regardless of preference configuration.
the app is good does all what is needed but i the connectivity with the server is bad content takes time to show or sometimes shows nothing at all.
some pages you try to go to dont load. i've refreshed, forced stopped the app. and still having issues not being able to see certain things
having trouble getting into the library it's saying oops something went wrong, i have delta force saved in the library but the library isn't loading
great for the steam guard. terrible for game browsing. it keeps closing on me while i'm browsing.
everythings good but sometimes the window glitches out and my steam profile isnt displayed (partially blacked out) even if it perfectly fine on my desktop version of the app
the only thing that bothers me is that some features causes the app to crash, for example trying to view your friend list/friends profiles

its good, but its a lil silly that this is specifically for your mobile and you still cant games you bought on steam that are specific mobile games without steam link. seems counter productive and why even have a mobile version?
i can't recover account because the verify email won't send. luckily my password hasn't changed :( i can now log in.

i am having problems with my steam app when trying to pay for a game. it keeps redirecting me to playstore to install gcash even when i already have it. i tried using different methods the outcome is still the same.
i love it the only problem is if i start downloading something and pause it and no matter what it will still download even if i pause it
it tells me to sign in even though i'm already signed in and gives me a server error when i try to "sign in"
family management requests so not work on mobile app. they are not visible once you click on them

maybe it doesn't matter to your development team, or you're too busy, but this app is a buggy mess compared to the desktop version. can't even go to the steam points shop and scroll sideways through the selection without it crashing to the home screen. i fully support you as a business, but when you have time, come and take a look at the app. thank you.
they should allow games in the steam library to sync with geforce now.
i downloaded this so i wouldn't have the waiting time when selling trading cards in the community, but it's still telling me i have to wait the 15 days.
every other day, there's some sort of glitch and it refuses to load the app.
library needs more than just seeing a big of your games with minimal filters
i rated 3 stars because when i went to google play to download this it said this app is unsupportable on this device i would love for this to be fixed
mostly fine but autoplaying videos and streams on mobile data is not good
it doesn't do anything. i have an account and it works on my windows pc. i have no idea why it doesn't work on my android tablet.
steam guard hasn't worked since january 28th. can't even buy games anymore.
ok in computer but not on phone, can't access main page says: "access denied"

good for easily searching the store, looking at your wishlist and rearranging it. unfortunately as of late you can't purchase anything. paypal continues to ask for your email and card won't fully accept and sits in the banks pending until it times out after a few days. items can be put in the basket but that's it now. used to work fine.
when try to purchase game or add funds to wallet via gcash, the app does not proceed to gcash app, instead goes to playstore and show gcash to download. please fix this.thank you

ok, not that bad, but doesn't work as good as previous app. also yeah, it needs steam guard code if you have it enabled. app for 2fa needs 2fa code that for many people is in this same app. way to lock people out of accounts. anyhow, this app is more modern, yes, but it is also bit slower than previous versions, but it at least works and decently streamlined. -3☆ for a potential of account lock out and +1 because app works and i kinda like the ui. result: 3/5 now bit worse than it was.

this app is amazing, can buy games and message steam people on the go wherever i am. the biggest problem i have is that i cannot gift a game to anyone using the steam mobile app, each time i try, it takes my money, and the transaction fails on steam's side. it is important to say that my bank eventually released the money that was intended for the game. if you fix that, i'll give you a sandwich.

even if this app should be rated 5 stars functional, the company has been indicted for digital price gouging. in addition, the 7 day "steam guard" 3fa trade lock is completely unnecessary for the majority of users. the steam website doesn't even allow you to stay signed in for more than a day, it keeps logging you off and spams asking you for if you are underage or not. vr only games on the steam games aren't labeled properly. for some reason the ui button for full screen is actually "big pi

they made the app probably because they had to, not because they wanted to. it forces you to be online every time you open the app, with no option to browse news, etc without drawing attention. it also tampers with your steam launcher status, which doesn't update even after you sign out/uninstall the app. with a quick google search i learnt that many such issues are years old, and they never cared to address those. i don't think they care enough about the mobile authenticator either.

when trying to sign in on mobile app, it tells me that i must authorise on my steam mobile app which is what i am trying to sign into. so have to go through long, ridiculous process of switch authoriser, type their email, put password, etc etc. half an hour later, i am still not have signed in. the security process is extremely long winded and unesscery. it puts me off from using steam. i just don't want to use it because of its complicity and because it's just not user friendly.

the app works for the basic stuff you'd expect, like steam guard, buying games, managing your wishlist and stuff like that, but it used to be so much better before the redesign. the new ui looks nicer but there are features missing (steam chat is no longer integrated into the app,now requires a separate app to use), and stuff like the library section is far more annoying to navigate, and you're not able to sort by stuff like playtime now by default, it switches to alphabetical every time. shame.

i'd like to recommend this, but it logged me out of my account this morning and i've been trying on and off all day to log back in, trying all sorts of recommended fixes, to no avail. honestly really offended, this app is decent when it works, but when it stops working it is the most infuriating thing, and it never seems to be anything done on the user's end.

it's very annoying that even though every setting i can find to get this app to send me push notifications when i have a new chat message, it remains completely silent. no ping, no notification in the pull down, nothing. please fix. i like chatting with my friends, but i can't do it effectively if i don't know they're talking to me or even online.

not bad update and i am sure will get used to it just can't understand why chat is separated. it's not bult in, instead is another app your you have to download first and multi accounts is not an option. you can't switch on the go between 2 steam profiles like in the chat app like in the main one. also widget is missing for the guard codes and in-app you are always prompted to scan qr code.

steam for phone works worse, some years ago it didn't make me join my account for phone but for pc it worked fine. sometimes it has some pretty convenient sales. but xbox and ps sales have lower costs (i spent 10€ a game in ps store instead of 30€ of steam). about pc it is pretty good, you can have your own library and put to favorites the games that you liked the most. it saves your games on your account and it's great (but for phone it works bad)

as you would expect. pretty much the same features as the desktop. pc is still alot better for reasons mentioned in other reviews. why i'm giving a 3 star is because the support for an issue i had was useless. they said the same thing twice and have told me to wait until september 4th, by which of then the offers for the games i tried to buy will no longer be available. it seriously seemed like they didn't even look at my screenshot properly. which isnt useful at all. honestly expected more.

years on, it still refuses to open the window to login to paypal. i have to reauthorize pp thru the webpage every couple months. recently, freezes more often while playing video. no matter what, i must re-confirm my age every time i open the app, in order to see games rated mature. minor bugs and edge cases get fixed on the regular, core features are left to rot. games delisted while in cart are unidentifiable. it's happened at least 3 times in the last year. in cart sunday, delisted monday.

the steam mobile app is a great tool for all steam users. it has great sync, is easy navigation, and is a must if you are into trading at all. my only gripes are that sometimes my friend's messages won't come through, forcing me to close and re-open the app for them to appear, and that this app could really use a visual overhaul, as it's long overdue now that they've given the official steam client a makeover.

before the recent "upgrade", i had the steam guard widget on my phone's home page. i could simply swipe between my profiles and see the authentication code in a single click. now, i no longer have a widget, and instead have to open the steam app, go to the steam guard page, choose my profile from a dropdown, click the "show steam guard code" button, then click a second "show steam guard code" button! would it be possible to bring back the steam guard widget? it was so much more convenient.

since the last update, i find it odd that whenever i click or select a game in the steam news section and other games featured in the catalog, it keeps on opening my browser. i installed the app because i don't want to use or look up games in thru the browser and now you're forcing it to the users. i think it's a bug. if it really is a bug, please fix it because it's quite annoying.

the app isn't bad. some login features can be tricky to use and the overall experience is somewhat worse than desktop. they fixed the ui issue with the queue. i'm sticking with 3 stars though because the app is clunky to use. it's not bad, but browsing sales, checking community pages, and looking at the market are all much worse on here. mostly things take longer to load but you'll have the occasional page error.

the library is showing me some valve games i never added. it's also showing me the hidden games. very frustrating! some ui features are broken, like trying to do a search while in the wishlist. or trying to go back. on the plus side, it's very good to browse, wishlist and buy games. i wouldn't put a bad review if they would listen to feedbacks, but when i tried messaging about these issues, it was like talking to a wall..

i recently tried to log in to steam mobile after some time, and it shows steam guard code to continue this login . how am i supposed to get this code if i'm trying to log in on the same device? i've tried many things to resolve this, but nothing seems to work. so many things to do, still everything is messed up, please help me to login in.

planned to buy some games during the sale. grunted that steam did not integrate with gpay. spent more time searching why. missed the sale, since ultimately, i did not care for the game enough to bother to remember digging out my cards. steam does not crosses the line for business i would trust with my cc info. close, but not quite. amazon pushing amazon pay alone was annoying enough, we don't need another corporate putting business interests above users. steam is an ant in comparison on top.

buying via the mobile app has become a chore; sometimes the app won't register the confirm payment step, other times it won't take you to card authentication , etc. the consequence of that is that people would sometimes miss out on their wishlisted games on sale while away from their pc. some other bugs persist as well, but none are so bothersome as this. edit: videos have become incredibly laggy even when fully loaded?
wish it would let me use my google play rewards money to add money to my account (as i only have that or cash it would be helpful so i can still make purchases)

the new ui looks good and easy to understand, but the app itself is now very, very slow and eats into ram and mobile data in the background like crazy. i often google a question about a game then find some steam discussion pages, tap them and the link opens in the app but it doesn't: the app just freezes on the starting screen (or it loads for longer than i'm willing to wait). also steam doesn't send guard notifications if the app isn't open.

opening this app is like spinning roulette, sometimes it will result in you seeing a blank screen, other times it works ok. and valve never seems in a hurry to fix it whenever the former happens. i use touch n' go as payment option, & it does weird stuff occasionally. it'd fail to detect payment & will notify you the payment isn't complete, even when you already authorised the payment & it's been deducted from my account.
its fine. doesnt work right now though. cant log in. please fix soon. its been out for a few weeks now.
it loads slow. but apart from that it seems to work fine. upi payments work which is very good. please add the feature of being able to view and manage collections in your library from the app.
language settings how english but in page it's in chinese

steam guard is nice and all but other features like the chats; they features on the pc and laptops that dont exist on mobile like nicknames, emotes, and stickers and the favorites tab. its hard for me to find that one friend who keeps changing their names every few days. its good point is that it has steam guard, everything works properly, and it isnt as buggy as steam on pc but the downside is that it lacks features.

i really liked the latest steam app update. it works, it shows notifications for wishlist and all. but there are still some issues that needs to be fixed. for example, a lot of the time when i'm scrolling in the specials menu looking for deals, the prices gets cut off the screen like there isn't enough screen space to show the price and it gets worse the more i scroll. it's like zoomed in somehow and i can't zoom out to see the price. please fix the issue otherwise the app is great! thank you.

anyone having issues signing up, use a web browser for the whole process - i'd advise downloading the app after you've created an account through the website, otherwise it will open the app again & put you back to the account creation page to start over. this app would be better if the client allowed you to play games on mobile from inside steam. dead app without the games.

app is ok, nothing more, nothing less. i hate it that i can't see my library the way i see on the desktop client, like a list. in this app, library is weird and not user-friendly. everytime i use the app, it seems like i am using a mobile version of the site, not the app itself. only thing i like about this app is wishlist, which is well done. other than that, app needs updating.

i'd love to say that it's "just like pc steam for mobile" but it's just not quite there yet. the store is really a mess on mobile and there's no way (yet) to use your google play account to buy games which would be really convenient for a mobile app. messaging friends works great and there aren't really any major bugs. the store just needs major fixes.

prettier than the last version but still seemingly useless..? i mean, it goes through the trouble of notifying me something on my wishlist is on sale, so i tap it, and the steam app opens...and thats it. doesn't go to the game page the notification was for, heck, doesn't even send me to my wishlist...it just opens the app... useless...but prettier than the last one otherwise would just be two stars...

largely useless. the only real reason to have it is for 2fa and the rest is unstable, inconvenient, unfinished, or just doesn't work. take for example viewing your own library. i can go to it just fine and view the games i own but i can't filter them and sorting by playtime only sorts by recent playtime which is pretty useless. i can use check it on the browser so it's not a big deal but my problem here is that when regular features are better on browser then it defeats the purpose of an app.

ok. this one is marginally better than the past app that was mostly used for steam guard. the new qr auth is seemlees and the new gui is nice but there's still a number of bugs that need work on. also, why did they separate steam chat from the app? it should be integrated back. and maybe add a favorite page to display what we want when the app is opened (obviously steam guard like always).

the rating is due to this app is a clunky and water down version of the main steam site. i love this app, it has a steam guard for protection, allows me to browse, trade in the marketplace, add to the wish lists, make steam guides, and etc. the downside is that ya need the steam chat app to get a better experience. lastly, some of the navigation is wonky, like even linking a curator's url in the forums has to be in a certain one way.

the app is ok, i would like to see valve put some time into polishing it. for instance, when you try to add a free game to your library, the app tells you to install steam... on your phone. hopefully the ui update for steam will bring the app up to date as well.

i'm giving it 3/5. it's a great app works fine. but if you want to send images/upload files then you need to use steam chat (the other app). this app allows you to chat to friends. however, it's very limited you can only text chat. if you want more you would need the stream chat app. i don't understand why the developers don't merge together the two apps to make one steam app! it's silly.

the confirm a trade on the app thingy doesn't work :(, also i can't activate game keys on the app. sometimes there are discord give-aways and it would be nice if i could quickly activate it using the app. edit*: it seems to have worked, but i constantly got an error message

i bought a game off of steam and after a little while i stopped playing. then when i started it up agian and hit continue i noticed something was off. so i checked my inventory, stats, and progress and it was like i hadn't even played it at all. and when i sent them a refund request and why i wanted a refund they said i couldn't get one because the purchase time exceeded the refund time. i checked the other games that i bought from steam and they were still at the same place i saved at last.

you get what you want. an application to manage your steam account and library. overall a 3 out of 5 due to a number of bugs and glitches. also some of the features like adding friends can be hard to find due to valve try to squish everything in one app. i would recommend this app if you use steam alot it will help with many thing and let you chat with friends on the go. the verification feature is nice as it saves alot of time. so don't bother to check this app out if your a steam gamer.

the app works... sometimes... i attempted to purchase a game via the app and it was stuck in a loop of me logging in, verifying with steam guard, needing to log in again and verifying over and over... truly a sisyphean task, simply purchasing a game... on the upside you *can* read funny steam reviews so that's neat.

the app is nice and all but the main reason why i'm using it (steam guard) isn't working properly. everytime i log into steam and require an auth code the app tries to do a push notification but instead it crashes. i can get the code by going to the steamguard option from within the app, but the crashes are damn annoying. to the app dev team: please fix this!

the only reason i downloaded this was to speed up my market sales by removing the hold time. however, going through all this stuff didn't remove the hold time anyway, so it's pointless unless you want to repost the 40 items again. what kind of sense does that make. my email has been plenty of security for 20+ years and this is supposed to be better? all it does is give a scammer another access point so it really doesn't make sense to have the app if your email hasn't been compromised.

this once was a great app, with every feature i needed to use steam on the go. i could view sales, find games, chat with friends, and overall kill some time when out and about. i didn't use it often, but i enjoyed using it. now however they have removed core features of the app such as the chat to separate programs for no good reason. when an app loses features and requires additional bloat to get those features back, i cannot rate it highly. but it does work fine mostly otherwise.

the store front is fine until you want to actually buy a game. paypal doesn't open the paypal verification, and it's the same when trying to pay with a card. seems to be a bug when switching apps for verification. app would get 1 star, but it gets 3 because it's saving me money by not allowing me to spend it in the first place 🙃 sort it out, valve. you're better than this.

i mostly use this to browse for new games, one big bummer that gets in the way of that is the inability to watch trailers/videos in fullscreen mode. pressing the super tiny fullscreen icon does nothing, and while flipping the phone over to see the app in landscape mode helps somewhat - it still doesnt get me into fullscreen mode, meaning i have to scroll to center the video as much as possible while sacrificing a chunk of the video because the blue title "header"(?) is in the way. annoying.

i love steam, and the app is pretty decent for the most part but there is a bug that has been persistent for at least 2 years at this point. whenever i click a link to a game or anything steam related and my phone opens the app it will always take me to the last thing i was viewing in the steam app (ie a game i was browsing, community post etc) i will then have to back out out the app and use the link again. it will usually work on the second try. drives me nuts!

the new app always redirected me to the 'splash' screen with 'what's new' presentation after logging in and trying to exit app using the 'back' button until force closed. it is also automatically launching camera when using 'guard' tab even when i don't want to scan qr code so it seems i must manually give my camera permission only when wanting to use camera instead of simply launching it on a button. but over-all i fancy easier way of logging in to my steam account.

new design is nice and all, but accessing my wishlist from the main screen is difficult (i can open the store dropdown and enter my wallet, but can't hit the "wishlist" button between them, so i have to navigate to it thru another menu. also, when i get a notification that something on my wishlist is on sale, tapping the notif does not take me to my wishlist, but to the store main page, where, again, i can't seem to tap on the wishlist button to get to it!

bad experience with app, i updated the app. somehow signed me out, i can't log in on the app because it has the authenticator, had to remove my phone number because i can't seem to get any text messages, had to go through all this process to reset my password and all of this fiasco when my phone should be the main app for logging in. fix the app by not automated of signing us out. dumb move valve, very inconvenient.

the old version was better imo, the layout was simple and easier to use. a helpful suggestion valve, add an option to change ui designs so that people who like the older version can continue using it and those who want the currenct/newer ones can use that instead. the unfortunality is that mobile isn't pc and some things should not have a pc layout. sure it may work better on a tablet, but who's carrying those around all the time?

not great. it contains several persistent bugs, including credit card verification. some missing or poorly implemented functions; the menu is confusing mess, and for reasons unknown, the app contains no interface for the steam chat. yes, there is a separate app for that, but man, let's not get into that rabbit hole. the app is usually quite responsive though (wrt connection speed and whatnot) and the steam guard system works ok, up until you lose or misplace your mobile phone.

so, valve won't let you use this app if you have a "locked account" meaning you haven't bought anything on their store for at least us$5, eventhough i created my account back in 2013 in ubuntu linux. i bought something, now this app works for me. seems useful for organizing your game titles, search and adding games to whislist or play later in desktop. i really don't find useful the steam link if i have to keep the pc with games on for that. overall, seems a decent app

i wish i could more easily see if a game was steamdeck compatible i have to go and look through a game's store page instead of just being able to see if a game supports steam deck or not on the list of supported platforms. also, i tend to get lost going around the ui i mean it takes me awhile to find my wishlist!

makes spending money on steam's pc gaming monopoly too easy. thankfully, the store's ui has bugged out, since 07/23, to where i can no longer see the price and discount amount from the list view because it continues off screen. a reinstall did not fix this issue. i'd have to open up each game page now to see how much it is. so, unless it's showcased on the top of pages, or in my wishlist ready to notify me of price changes, i'm saving money.

good app that let's you do most things could want from phone. only issues i find are most times you actually try to buy anything in app the purchase just fails. it's not as easy to add games to collections and a few other things (basically if can right click on the pc app, a hold should bring up similar options on mobile in my view) and the fact you can't listen to your owned soundtracks, meaning that any ost buy you still need to download & run via a 3rd party platform instead which is a shame.

the platform is decent for buying games, currently owning at around 380 games, all of which i love to play! but the biggest issue the software has and is a recurring problem is their weekly maintenance day, what was originally just tuesdays at around 4pm pst has now resulted in a maintenance that goes off twice and completely breaks //everything// on both tuesdays and wednesdays around the same time, so don't really rely on this as a source of communication, use discord or other social platforms

can i get an explanation of why mt account isn't being accepted, especially when i followed all procedures when signing up in a new account? i'm new and trying to get my first ever steam account, what's going on? it literally takes me back to the sign up page every time i: added my email address, then confirmed it, check marked the agreements, pressed continue then takes me back, explain please i need your assistance.

the app is great and makes a good companion to the pc app. it's too bad it take 20 hours to download anything on steam. no matter how fast your internet is, what the download limit is, or if on eathernet or wi-fi. expect to wait a very long time for anything you pay for.

scrolling from left to right too fast hard to scroll right , when games is on wishlist can't see 40% of the image becaus wishlist word takes almost all of the space of the game picture and can't see title almost of the timeit should be only a small star to tell is on wishes ... scrolling too fast and skipping some titles i'm only suggesting what could be better to help you ... i know the app isn't old so its a good start btw ... i'm using it on a s20fe and it could have some minors fixes !

despite the fancy new ui, it could be better. sometimes trying to add items from the wishlist into the cart won't work, so you'll have to go directly to the game's page to do so. the way steam gift cards/funds are added is the desktop ui so it feels a little strange, and there's no landscape mode for android tablets. also, steam chat is a separate app, for some reason.

works great. used as a 2fa for your steam accounts. wish they would update the chat system to reflect that of the desktop version instead of releasing it's own separate app for that. especially since if you're logged into the steam app the same time as the chat app, you get double notifications for every message you receive.

very useful app if you want to browse the steam store or manage your game library on the go. unfortunately i can't rate higher because every single time i click a link in the browser or another app to a steam game that opens this app, it opens on the homepage and i have to navigate back to the link and click it again before it will go to the games page. please fix this.

yeah, this isn't the way to use steam. it's nice to receive notifications on your phone about sales and do some basic stuff, but the external links are always broken and the layout is terrible, is very hard to get to places especially, if you're used to the pc port and it takes too long to load anything, and everyday you use it you discover a new error to why nothing is loading. if you use steam, you probably have a pc, just use that version, this is just a nice convenience.

i was trying to create an account from my phone because that is an option. i did everything correctly, confirming email address and checking the box, but when i hit the continue button, it erased everything i had inputted and made me do it again, and again, and again. if the developer bothers to look at this, please fix this issue. edit: i was able to create a profile on my phone, but i had to got to the website. i personally think that, since it is a option, you should be able to on the app.

could be so much better! notifications often don't take me to the right page, scrolling the store has lots of listings cut off at weird places, thumbnails take forever to load on the wishlist. does the job but there's a lack of care that makes the mobile app sloppy. a little bit of extra time and effort could make a huge difference.

i mean its good for installing or starting things in your pc remotely but almost 90% of my attempts into log in with the scanning qr option it just says "something went wrong" and then i have to wait 30minutes to attempt again. i don't play anymore bc i dont have time and this (something that's made for save you time) only makes it worse fr

the steam client was amazingly fun and convenient in its day, but in 2025 it's now censored on the game stores. even with content age verification enabled in your preferences, you'll still encounter blurred images and blacked-out previews. interestingly, the pc web browser version of steam remains unaffected and functions as intended. for regular browsers of adult games, this glitch presents a significant inconvenience. continuing with the test on steam deck...

has improved some. old; must give personal information in order to purchase, even using steam account money. also, no real support at all. very difficult to even try getting a support ticket created. yes, a support ticket! how dumb, maybe just hire someone to help your customers!!!!!!!!

very laggy and often doesnt load, but most needed features work perfect, like the steam gaurd, confirmations, and notifications for sales. buying/browsing games works aight, just pretty laggy and takes time to load, sometimes just not loading at all inventory, friend reqs, and especially chat do not work. like at all.

valve has neglected this app, the only real reason we use this application is for steam guard. for starters the ui is very lacking and there are some issues with confirming actions on steam; after accepting the pages stay and pressing either yes or no again will give an error unless you manually refresh the page. this app seems to be a glorified but clunky web browser to confirm actions on your steam account and for using steam guard.

nice being able to view steam on my phone, although i probably wont use it much since i can't link my google play account to the steam wallet. would be nice to have some functionality for that, was pretty disappointed that steam on your phone doesn't mean buying steam games with something other than a debit card. app overall is meh as others have pointed out.

i like steam and the app works as expected but this is the only place i can find to post a review and maybe be heard. downloading games is inconsistent at best i'll have spikes where i get good speeds followed by an equal amount of time where it completely stops. and they force the updates before you can launch the game so if you're playing a game like ark especially when running mods you cant connect to a server that hasn't been updated

i downloaded the app primarily for the forums access vs using my phone's browser, but there appear to be some basic functions and qol options missing, such as refresh, being able to get the link to a thread for pasting in another, or even just being able to easily and directly access the forums for the game you want. it also tends to reset you back to the main store page after being out of it for a time, making that last point a bit more of a pain.

3 stars because it works and doesn't crash anymore. the old app was much better. easier to navigate, but most importantly everything was in one place, including the chat and friends list, which is now separate. the previous app was more like steam itself - this one looks like a simplified phone version.

while the new ui brings a fresh new face with quick shortcut icons always available, the inability to chat is a major downside. you either have to jump through hoops to access chats or be prompted to download the redundant steam chat app. wish there was a way to revert to the old style of one wished to do so.

there's two features i actually want in this app 1: the ability to play steam games on android. i don't expect to get wolfenstein on here but plenty of older games could run here. i'm not referring to streaming a game from the pc. 2: the ability to download steam games for later transfer to a pc, because sometimes we have more spare data than time

chat still not realtime after all this time. steam app always have some delay, even after the chat already appeared on the desktop app. most of the time, i need to manually refresh the chat to see my friend's messages. sometimes, the messages come in large droves on the cellphone app. the chat notification is similarly delayed, and therefore, useless.

i was looking forward to the steam app being finally updated but unfortunately it's not very good at all.. the whole app feels much slower and is more or a pain to use than previous versions. i don't understand why you have completely removed the ability to chat from the app forcing us to download another useless app to our phones just to allow us to chat ! please re add this functionality there was no reason for it to be removed in the first place other than to advertise your other buggy app.

this app was great to chat with friends when not at home, but not anymore. with one of the newest update the chat function no longer works without downloading a separate app (which it sends me to the play store to download when i click the chat button in this app), kinda dumb. the only way to use this app to chat is to use some roundabout way through this apps friends list that opens up a non mobile friendly chat box.

glorified browser, with not much effort put into user experience. i tend to get stuck in cart checkout pages, browsing forums is a pain cos the text won't display properly, the videos in the game store pages don't go fullscreen for some reason. it is just janky all round - i guess i only have it installed to be notified of sales and mobile authentication. for actually closing purchases i end up opening my pc since this app never works.

i've had steam since middle school (despite not having been on it until 2022) and it worked fine, now i'm having issues with installing games despite having great internet connection, same with pausing/stopping installations and uninstalling...it takes a good bit to do both. idk if it's just my pc or it's steam but the app's stability needs to be fixed

needs more mobile friendly options. mobile data usage is insane, 1.5gb just for browsing a few games, seems video preload is too aggressive and should have options to default to lower resolution videos or skip preloading altogether. also needs a setting to stop the app from logging into steam chat when openened. some parts of the ui (chat specially) look dated

i think interface could be much easier to use, sometimes it feels like what i change in the app doesn't really work also confirmation doesn't disappear when u do confirm it works but when u try to click it again it disappears with a message that confirmation failed anyways the things could be much smoother and beautiful it feels as if the app was created in 2014 and never got an update

steam is a very good platform for gamers. they obviously care a lot about their community and they are very hopeful when your account gets hacked. however i would not know that because it is unnecessarily difficult and hard to sign up for steam. i literally had to make a new gmail account and even then still for some reason the security programs of it make it difficult for me to sign in on my laptop it's ridiculous

the store is easy to navigate, and product screens are well structured. three issues, though: 1. the back functionality is inconsistent and doesn't behave like i feel it should 2. opening steam links from other apps will often just open the last page i was on, rather than the target page. i think this happens more when the app hasn't been used in 24 hours 3. when paying for purchases, the 3ds flow feels janky, and i don't get enough feedback that my purchase has been completed successfully.

pathetic services. my account was stolen, and i made a support ticket. no one is getting back to me, and it's been more than a week. i am getting messages of my account being sold, but i can't do anything because steam support just doesn't work. my account is worth a lot. i do not recommend using steam for any type of purchase.

please add the group chats from the steam chats section. as of now i can just chat with individual players. the app also tries to maintain the same color scheme and profile as that of the steam pc app but it looks quite dull. also the button response for various actions, especially steam confirmations, is weird: you click it and it doesn't give you any feedback but the action is done: neither the button highlights while processing nor do i have a toast/notification. get's the job done though.

it's alright... works well enough i guess. it's quite barebone and has some issues. valve has a weird way of doing half of the work and just release it like that. as a company it seem to lack structure and routines. i like valve, it does cool stuff. but buggy and slow clients are nothing new. :(

ok but need some work, navigation is difficult, the store pages is not really mobile friendly, searchin is cumbersome and gameplay videos / trailers either do not play or the controls are impossible to use and fullscreen is broken. i hope valve give the app some polish it needs to be a useful companion not a awkward option.

honestly a useful app, but in serious need of actual work. the layout and ui of the app are awful and it's just like a browser for the computer copied and pasted onto the phone. overlapping buttons and many features missing make this app nothing more then a tool for steam guard/away from steam. it's worth the install but needs a remake.

the steam client for mobile devices is what you'd expect: relatively lightweight, provides access to the steam store, marketplace, and the community as a whole. if you have the pc client, this is easily the app you would have while on-the-go. this was a very convenient client to especially chat with friends on the platform, until the latest update, where the chat function was removed and placed into its own app. this hurts the app, and keeps it from being any higher than 3/5.

steam mobile app's ui doesn't seem to be optimized for mobile usage, complex drop down menus and tiny buttons feels almost like a copy/paste from the windows application. other than the navigation and ui steam sometimes seems to not load on my broadband but works fine on mobile data, this only happens rarely. plus, give us actual post game session content like the windows app does. tracking achievements in the app works just fine and buying new games and content also works fine for me.

urghhh i was hoping that the new version of the app wasn't web-wrapped as the old one - turns out it is. please stop trying to save money or whatever the argument is for not building a pure native app. the potential of the content you guys want to present in the app is threwn away by not building the app using a framework that allows for high performance output. app now still feels like a (slow) mobile website even though it might look a bit better than before. swiftui + jetpack compose = 5stars

steam is great, i use it for my computer a lot, but this mobile app is absolute garbage. i couldn't even sign into my account, it kept resetting the page every time i went to my email to check the verification code. it also would tell me my password is wrong, when it was reset 5 times, and all times was the correct password. please fix this, its a little annoying

steam should develop cloud gaming apps that are completely free to use. it doesn't make sense for users to purchase games on steam and then still have to pay for playtime on cloud gaming platforms. this additional cost can be a significant burden for gamers who have already invested in their game libraries.

"there was an error with your search, please try again later" i have to refresh it and maybe even close the app and open the market page again just because of that. please fix this, its annoying.

disgusting attitude towards your product. alas, there is no alternative to this. always, absolutely always faced with certain problems in steam. the application on the phone for some reason refuses to log into the account, at the moment. for some reason yesterday, the "steam goard" page did not work at all, and therefore i could not link my account to the phone. the inventory is in the game, god forbid, if it is loaded every three days.

love the new interface and easy of use! navigating used to be a chore and i avoided using the old, but you are definately moving in the right direction. one thing i would recommend, is maybe give the store (and other pages using the blue desktop theme) a mobile stylesheet to fit in with the app. the thing that really annoys me though, is that the chat is in a seperate app, why do this!? this is a four to five star app, but without chat it goes down to 3 stars for me. anyway, well done team!

app is really garbage. tells me of wishlist items, and when you click on them, it starts to load the page, then tells you page cannot be displayed. old version worked better. wish i could go back. i really don't know why we post reviews, they never fix anything.

this app needs a big update as much as steam itself does. lots of things dont work or are incredibly frustrating to do. and why now when i try to buy something trough the app it opens a browser to log in to my bank, but then does not return to app but just goes to steam web page where im not logged in and the transaction fails? and that happens randomly, not all the time... how hard it its to make an webapp that dosent just open a browser when you click on something...

helps acquire games in a free way (not a road). not one of the games are available through google. not advertising stadia any more, for i have no idea what reason. now it suggests a steam deck, while i use a phone. the opinion of some other critics that steam has no idea who the market audience seems true to me. at least it stopped advertising to robots, but it now wants money from game or movie characters? what happened to start that problem? they are characters, so i get nft as an idea.

has potential to be better, missing a lot of good features, remote play should be integrated into the steam app itself to allow remote access to any connected pc's from anywhere, similar to xbox remote play, social notifications and easier navigation through the app.

useless, if you don't have you phone you can't sign back into your account using email or whatever. don't use this. this is dumb as f why don't you just let us sign in using both email code and mobile code????? the stupidest idea ever. when you lost you phone, you can no longer have access to your account. update** i got my account back. steam help response time is pretty fast. make sure everyone, to save your purchase codes. i use it to recover! steam support : charlotte. thanks bro.
